rules.detail: Block new entries where real rate differential favors the opposite direction. This is the macro engine's core signal — trading against it is anti-edge.
Macro signal may be right directionally but momentum is still against us. Wait for momentum crossover or reduce size aggressively.
Reduce position size to 0.5× when entering a flat, low-volatility market. Trailing stops are premature in compression; wait for expansion.
Compressed volatility means the pair cannot deliver the range needed to hit targets. This is separate from the ICT ADR filter — apply at signal generation too.
When rate differential is too narrow (<50bp real), the macro edge disappears. Skip these setups or wait for CB divergence to widen.
When real rate divergence is extreme (>250bp), the directional edge is highest. Scale to 1.25× base size.

rules.condition: real_rate_diff aligns with direction AND |real_rate_diff| >= 2% AND momentum confirms
rules.detail: Strong macro alignment with momentum confirmation is the system's highest-confidence setup. Scale to 1.5× base size.

rules.condition: exit_type would be trailing_stop AND ATR_14d > 0.9%
rules.detail: High-ATR trending markets let trailing stops run. Widen trailing stop multiplier from 1.0× to 1.3× ATR in these conditions.
